page: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel x86_64 processor based on the Haswell architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz, with hyperthreading disabled. The system is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by the stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations, along with Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, consistently perform in the middle 50% tier.

Given the dedicated vCPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the b2-120 offers a stable, predictable resource profile for multi-threaded workloads. The trade-off lies in its older Haswell architecture and lower clock speed, which limits its suitability for single-threaded or latency-sensitive tasks. Qualitatively, it represents a cost-efficient option for parallelizable workloads that do not require modern instruction sets like AVX-512. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
description: The OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Intel x86_64 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz with hyperthreading disabled. It is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, a local 400 GB SSD, and a 10 Gbps baseline network interface. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct trade-off: single-core processing is weak, placing in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently achieve average, mid-tier results. Lacking modern instruction sets like AVX-512 and GPU acceleration, this server is not designed for heavy AI text generation. Instead, its dedicated resource allocation and balanced hardware profile make it a qualitatively cost-effective solution for parallelized workloads, including multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD. It delivers reliable mid-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-120 is a general-purpose server with 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD, offering balanced multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core server featuring balanced memory and local SSD storage, optimized for parallelized general-purpose workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel x86_64 cores with hyperthreading disabled
120 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local SSD storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Well-suited for multi-threaded compilation, databases, and web serving
